exercises.prompt: Complete correctly: '___ padres viven en Madrid.' (my)
Es un amigo ___. (mine — use the long form after the noun)

exercises.incorrectSentence: Me lavo mis manos antes de comer.
exercises.correctSentence: Me lavo las manos antes de comer.

exercises.note: Long-form possessives agree in gender and number with the thing possessed — casa is feminine, so nuestro becomes nuestra.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite to remove the ambiguity of su, specifying that the book belongs to her:
exercises.instruction: replace su with de + the correct prepositional pronoun

exercises.prompt: Match each short-form possessive to its long-form equivalent:
Fill in the blanks with the correct possessive:
exercises.passage: {0} hermana vive con {1} marido en Sevilla. La casa es {2}, no de sus padres.
